title: Destruction of Despayre
content:

Alliance]]'' *[[Attack on the first Death Star]][[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] |conc= |next=*[[Mission to Danuta]]''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' *[[Skirmish in the Horuz system]] |image=[[File:Destruction_of_Despayre-TEA.jpg]] |name=Destruction of Despayre |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]{{Film|IV}} |date=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places the [[attack on the first Death Star]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], so everything between that battle and the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] must fall in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].{{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}} |place=[[Despayre]] |result=Imperial victory *Despayre and countless prisoners are destroyed, and the Death Star's power is tested |side1=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |side2=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=[[Rianna Saren]] |commanders2=[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*Rianna Saren *[[Z-58-0|Zeeo]] |forces2=*[[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]]''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]'' *[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *Security droids *[[Probe droid/Legends|Probe droids]] *[[Scout trooper/Legends|Scout troopers]] *[[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1= |casual2=*Stormtroopers *Security droids *2 Probe droids *Scout troopers *Imperial officers |casual3= |casual4= |civilian=The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Despayre and all life on its surface }} {{Quote|Engineering tells me that we can manage thirty percent power and, after a fast capacitor recharge for an hour or two, that much again.''"
"''How strong will that beam be?''"
"''Theoretical. Nobody knows for sure.''"
"''Well, then we need to test it before we embark.''"
"''That would be wise. Do you have a target in mind?''"
"''Yes. I do.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]] and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]|}} The '''Destruction of Despayre''' was a cataclysmic event that occurred in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], following the [[attack on the first Death Star]]. The [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Despayre]] was the final construction site of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], where assembly of the [[Space station/Legends|space station's]] [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] took place. After discovery of the site by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the project's leader, [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], decided to move the station. The interior was near completion and the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was functional, but the Grand Moff decided it was necessary to test the superlaser before getting under way. A proper test firing required a terrestrial world, and the penal colony was the only suitable location in [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] range. Tarkin decided without hesitation to sacrifice Despayre, and a few days before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the planet was destroyed. The destruction of Despayre was one of only three recorded uses of the Death Star's superlaser. The incident caused a shift in the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] configuration of the [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]], and was a major [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]], as millions of lives were [[Death/Legends|terminated]] almost instantly. The shock was felt by [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], both trained and untrained. ==History== ===Preparation=== {{Quote|For ''practice''? Just to see how well it'll work?|[[Tenn Graneet]]|}} [[File:Despayre.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Prior to its destruction, Despayre was a world covered with life.]] For approximately two years, the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]], more commonly known as the Death Star, had undergone construction in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Prison/Legends|penal]] colony [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Despayre]], a site chosen for its location in a distant corner of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] where the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] and [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] media would be unlikely to observe.''[[The Movie Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' [[Slavery/Legends|Slaves]] from all over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] had labored long and hard in order to finish construction on [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] newest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. Leading up to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] launched progressively grander efforts to stall the project. The Rebels captured the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Priam (starship)|''Priam'']] to learn the location of the construction site, and then sent the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class carrier]] ''[[Fortressa]]'' on a [[Attack on the first Death Star|mission]] to destroy the Death Star, which led to the first operational use of the station's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]]—bringing a swift end to the Rebel assault. Following this, [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], the overall leader of the Death Star project, made the decision to relocate the station. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]], the station's operational commander, concurred. However, Tarkin wished to test the functionality of the superlaser in a more controlled fashion than its first firing. Tarkin himself chose the site for the superlaser's first controlled test. The [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]] had been largely emptied by the construction project's resource-gathering efforts, leaving Despayre itself as the only suitable target within [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] range. The decision stunned personnel across the entire station, from [[Superlaser Fire Control]] to Admiral Motti himself. But ultimately, the decision was Tarkin's, and no one sought to challenge it. In fact, Tarkin had long predicted that Despayre was doomed anyway,[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] as a cloud of industrial debris was already benighting the planet[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 74|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 74]] and would have remained in orbit long after the construction ended. When Tarkin made the decision to move the station, the Death Star's engineering division wasn't quite prepared for a full-power discharge from the superlaser's main capacitors. A 33% charge was the best they were willing or able to manage. They knew 4% power was sufficient to destroy a ''Lucrehulk''-class battleship, but no one could be certain what kind of damage the superlaser could inflict at one-third of its full capacity. What was certain, however, was that even at that strength, the first shot would [[Death/Legends|kill]] millions of prisoners. The duty of firing the shot fell to [[Tenn Graneet]], the [[Master chief petty officer|chief]] of the [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] crew for that shift. The [[Captain/Legends|commanding officer]] of Superlaser Fire Control was willing to allow alternate shift leaders [[Beller]] or [[Reshias]] to substitute for Graneet, but ultimately Graneet chose carry out the order himself. [[File:Despayre3.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Death Star orbits Despayre, shortly before firing the third superlaser shot.]] As Tarkin considered any non-essential personnel a security risk, anyone whose usefulness ended with the termination of construction was [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttled]] to the planet. Prisoner work crews were returned to their compounds, and construction supervisors and other personnel were transported back to Despayre as well. As they awaited reassignment, they were unaware of their impending demise. One of those prisoners was [[Rianna Saren]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Mission to Tatooine (Rianna Saren)|captured]] by the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] slaver [[Zarien Kheev]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. After being brought to the planet, however, Saren quickly orchestrated her escape. Using her concealed weapon, a spring-knife called the [[Thorn of Ryloth]], she killed the stormtroopers guarding her and tried to find a way off planet. Saren soon found her droid companion, [[Z-58-0|Zeeo]], in a trash compactor and met another Twi'lek slave, who informed her about a nearby air duct, which could be used in her escape. After surviving an encounter with a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]], Saren and Zeeo reached the surface of Despayre through the air duct and saw the Death Star orbiting the planet. After killing some [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]] stationed on the surface, the Twi'lek stole a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] and flew it to the landing pad used by [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]], which carried Imperial personnel and supplies to and off planet. On the landing pad, Saren once again encountered Kheev, who shot at Zeeo before escaping on one of the shuttles. Not willing to stop her pursuit of Kheev, Saren, along with her droid, hid in a shipment crate, which was picked up by another shuttle and was then brought to the Death Star itself. After reaching the Death Star, Saren continued her search for Kheev, defeating many stormtroopers and another probe droid along the way. ===Destruction=== {{Quote|You may fire when ready.|Grand Moff Tarkin|}} [[File:DespayreDestruction.jpg|thumb|left|The Death Star's superlaser destroys Despayre.]] The first one-third power shot from the superlaser was a planet-killer in itself. Though it didn't physically destroy Despayre, it did terminate all life on the surface. The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] itself became a massive firestorm, and the planet's ground [[Groundquake/Legends|became unstable]] from the blast. The tectonic plates shifted, causing massive waves in the planet's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] and extreme volcanic activity. Tarkin was pleased with the results. When Motti questioned the destruction of one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] primary prison colonies, Tarkin dismissed it with the expectation that the Empire would employ the death penalty more frequently. One hour and fifteen minutes after the first shot, the Death Star fired a second time. This shot further intensified the seismic effects, causing [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] to rise and fall in a matter of minutes and the surface to be covered with oceans of [[Magma/Legends|lava]]. Despayre had gone from a verdant world of deep green, broken by rivers and seas,''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' to a lifeless sphere of [[red]]. The final shot was fired one hour and nineteen minutes after the second. When the final shot hit, Despayre shattered into billions of fragments ranging from pebble- to mountain-sized. The planet no longer existed as a single entity in terms of the [[Star system/Legends|star system's]] [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] configuration. The Death Star itself recorded the impact of thousands of fragments against the station's [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], none of which caused damage worth mentioning. At the same time, Rianna Saren had at last found Kheev, who had been talking to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Hiding in the shadows, the Twi'lek overheard as Vader ordered Kheev to leave for the planet [[Danuta]] with the updated [[Death Star plans/Legends|battle station plans]], which had been requested by the engineers there. After witnessing the destruction of Despayre first-hand, Saren decided to inform the Rebellion about the Death Star and the threat it presented. ===Aftermath=== {{Quote|Any attack made by the Rebels against this station would be a useless gesture, no matter what technical data they've obtained.|Admiral Motti|}} [[File:DespayreGoBoom-XW.png|thumb|right|200px|The remains of Despayre float in space.]] Aboard the Death Star, untrained [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Nova Stihl]] awoke screaming. Darth Vader felt the planet's destruction while the [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']], his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], was in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] en route to the construction site. He knew only that it was a terrible [[Wound in the Force/Legends|wound in the Force]], until the ship dropped out of hyperspace and saw the expanding debris field on its scopes. The planet had been home to countless plant and animal [[Species/Legends|species]], and was the only habitable world in the Horuz system. Any native species that had not been transplanted would have been exterminated. Some personnel aboard the Death Star, civilian contractors especially, were shocked. Others, especially military personnel whose careers were heavily invested in the project, like Admiral Motti, considered the planet's destruction to be vindication of their faith in the project. Meanwhile, Rianna Saren managed to reach Death Star's communication center and contacted Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], one of the major supporters of the Rebellion. Saren informed the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] about the battle station's destructive capabilities and, realizing that the Empire was her true enemy, not Kheev, asked Organa to send her to Danuta along with the [[Kyle Katarn|other]] Rebel agents to steal the battle station plans. After that, Saren and Zeeo escaped the Death Star using a stolen Imperial ship. Little did Saren know that near the same time, the Rebel Alliance had sent [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to follow up after the ill-fated ''Fortressa''. However, when Rebel forces comprising the two [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] of ''[[Ethar]]'' group arrived in the region, they found only the planet's remains. The Death Star was absent and the Rebels were captured by the Imperials that remained in the system. It was only thanks to the skills of [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] that those ships were later retrieved by the Alliance. The destruction of Despayre provided the operational testing necessary for the engineering division to clear the superlaser for full-power operation, and a short time later a single shot at full capacity would be used to [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroy]] the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. ==Appearances== *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] *''[[Star Wars: Lethal Alliance]]'' *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] {{1st}} *[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' *[[Death Star Technical Companion (Second Edition)|''Death Star Technical Companion'', Second Edition]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *{{FactFile|74|CRE 1-4|Creation of the First Death Star}} *''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy]]'' *{{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}} *[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] *[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 9|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 9]] *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.45|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' Comic 6.45]] *''[[Death Star Owner's Technical Manual]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Skyhook}} {{Interlang |es=Destrucción de Despayre }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Despayre, Destruction of}} [[Category:Artificial disasters]] [[Category:Battles in the Horuz system]] [[Category:Battles of Operation Skyhook]] [[Category:Genocides and massacres]]
